threshold_count: At least two clearly individual personalities, each with unique attributes
duration_rule: Not specified; typically chronic and beginning in childhood

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a disruption of identity characterized by the presence of two or more distinct personality states, each with its own pattern of perceiving and interacting with the environment.
The patient experiences recurrent memory gaps for everyday events, personal information, or traumatic events that exceed ordinary forgetfulness.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Inability to recall important autobiographical information, usually of a stressful or traumatic nature
duration_rule: Variable; can range from minutes to many years

symptoms.description: The patient is unable to recall important autobiographical information, usually of a traumatic or stressful nature, that is too extensive to be explained by ordinary forgetfulness.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Inability to recall personal history plus sudden, purposeful travel or bewildered wandering
duration_rule: Variable; often brief (hours or days), but can be longer

symptoms.description: The patient is unable to recall important autobiographical information, usually of a traumatic or stressful nature, that is too extensive to be explained by ordinary forgetfulness.
The patient experiences apparently purposeful travel or bewildered wandering associated with amnesia for their identity or other important autobiographical information.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persistent or recurrent episodes of feeling detached from one's self or surroundings while reality testing remains intact

symptoms.description: The patient experiences depersonalization, feeling detached from or as if they are an outside observer of their own mental processes or body.
The patient experiences derealization, perceiving their surroundings as unreal, dreamlike, distant, or distorted.
The patient maintains intact reality testing during episodes of depersonalization or derealization.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Symptoms characteristic of a dissociative disorder that cause distress but do not meet full criteria for any specific disorder
duration_rule: Variable (e.g., acute reactions last less than 1 month)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits symptoms characteristic of a dissociative disorder that fall below the threshold for a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Dissociative symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits dissociative symptoms, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
